clusters.title: My priority is to take Sri Lanka to top of the tree in world cricket – Kirsten

versions.title: My priority is to take Sri Lanka to top of the tree in world cricket – Kirsten
versions.deck: After a considerable period, Sri Lanka cricket has secured a top-quality coach to handle the national team in the form of Gary Kirsten, whose proven record speaks for itself, having guided India to win the 2011 Cricket World Cup, beating Sri Lanka in the final, and having taken his native South Africa to the top of the ICC Test rankings.

clusters.title: Govt. says President has not made request to remove SLC Head

versions.title: Govt. says President has not made request to remove SLC Head
versions.deck: The Government and Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) have said that they are not aware that President Anura Kumara Dissanayake has asked SLC President Shammi Silva to step down to facilitate the appointment of an interim committee.

clusters.title: Yamal scores but injured as Barcelona beat Celta to restore La Liga lead

versions.title: Yamal scores but injured as Barcelona beat Celta to restore La Liga lead
versions.deck: Lamine Yamal was injured while converting a penalty in Barcelona’s Spanish league win against Celta Vigo.

clusters.title: Sri Lanka XV brace for Kiwi Test ahead of Asia Rugby Top 4

versions.title: Sri Lanka XV brace for Kiwi Test ahead of Asia Rugby Top 4
versions.deck: Sri Lanka XV will face a stern test against the visiting New Zealand Under-85kg side in a two-match series designed to sharpen the national outfit ahead of next month’s Asia Rugby Top 4 tournament.

clusters.title: Real Madrid close on Barcelona as Mbappe and Vinicius net against Alaves

versions.title: Real Madrid close on Barcelona as Mbappe and Vinicius net against Alaves
versions.deck: Kylian Mbappe and Vinicius Junior each scored as Real Madrid ended a four-game winless streak by defeating Alaves 2-1 to move closer to Spanish league leader Barcelona.

clusters.title: Royal College Rugby Season 2026 kicks off

versions.title: Royal College Rugby Season 2026 kicks off
versions.deck: Royal College, Colombo, is set to launch the Inter-Schools Rugby Tournament on 26 April 2026, against Science College. The much-anticipated Under-19 rugby season is just around the corner, with leading schools preparing to deliver an exciting and competitive tournament for rugby enthusiasts.

clusters.title: Brandix props up with Royal Rugby as Platinum Sponsor

versions.title: Brandix props up with Royal Rugby as Platinum Sponsor
versions.deck: Royal College has officially launched its 2026 rugby season with strong backing from an impressive list of sponsors, reaffirming the school’s status as one of Sri Lanka’s leading rugby powerhouses. Brandix had shown confidence in the Reid Avenue outfit with their signing as the Platinum sponsor for the 2026 rugby season.

clusters.title: Mckyle leads Thomians at Rugby

versions.title: Mckyle leads Thomians at Rugby
versions.deck: S. Thomas’ College, Mount Lavinia will be led by Number 8, Mckyle Karunaratne for the 2026 rugby season with high hopes of restoring their proud rugby legacy and bringing glory back to the school. With a rich sporting tradition spanning 175 years, Thomian rugby remains one of the most respected names in school rugby.

clusters.title: Jetour takes field as sponsor of historic Big Match encounters in 2026

versions.title: Jetour takes field as sponsor of historic Big Match encounters in 2026
versions.deck: Jetour, the global SUV brand represented locally by Euro Motors Ltd., strengthened ties with communities as sponsor of three prestigious school cricket encounters in March.

clusters.title: Janashakthi Life strengthens school cricket legacy through Battle of the Hindus partnership

versions.title: Janashakthi Life strengthens school cricket legacy through Battle of the Hindus partnership
versions.deck: Janashakthi Life, served as the Title Partner for the 15th edition of the Battle of the Hindus, as Hindu College Colombo and Jaffna Hindu College renewed their proud school cricket rivalry, bringing together students, old boys, and supporters in celebration of a long-standing sporting tradition.

clusters.title: Iraj Pathiraja appointed new Immigration Controller General

versions.title: Iraj Pathiraja appointed new Immigration Controller General
versions.deck: The Cabinet of Ministers has approved the appointment of Iraj Chaminda Pathiraja, an officer in the Special Grade of the Sri Lanka Administrative Service (SLAS), as the Controller General of Immigration and Emigration on a permanent basis.

clusters.title: Steering senior public sector officials towards innovative thinking

versions.title: Steering senior public sector officials towards innovative thinking
versions.deck: Recognising the indispensable role of a robust public sector in achieving national development goals, Citra, Sri Lanka’s pioneering Social Innovation Lab, established as a joint initiative between the Prime Minister’s Office and the United Nations Development Program (UNDP) in Sri Lanka, together with the Presidential Secretariat of Sri Lanka and the Sri Lanka Institute of Development Administration (SLIDA), recently hosted the fifth “Citra Fello

clusters.title: Aventude wins top honours for engineering excellence

versions.title: Aventude wins top honours for engineering excellence
versions.deck: Aventude has secured a major win at the Singapore Business Review Technology Excellence Awards 2026, taking home the award for Information Management – Computer Software in recognition of its outstanding engineering contribution to Norway’s public sector digitalisation through the low code platform, Compose.

clusters.title: Dialog Enterprise partners Star Garments: Pioneering 5G innovation in Sri Lanka’s apparel industry

versions.title: Dialog Enterprise partners Star Garments: Pioneering 5G innovation in Sri Lanka’s apparel industry
versions.deck: Dialog Enterprise, the corporate solutions arm of Dialog Axiata PLC, has partnered with Star Garments Ltd., to enable next-generation textile manufacturing through advanced 5G-powered solutions, enhancing operational efficiency, automation and real-time decision-making across its production ecosystem.

clusters.title: Bank of Ceylon reinforces national economic vision with 2025 Annual Report presentation

versions.title: Bank of Ceylon reinforces national economic vision with 2025 Annual Report presentation
versions.deck: In a significant moment reflecting renewed confidence in Sri Lanka’s economic recovery and forward-looking national strategy, the Bank of Ceylon (BOC) formally presented its 2025 Annual Report to President Anura Kumara Dissanayake. The occasion reaffirmed the bank’s role as the nation’s leading financial institution and a key pillar of economic stability.

clusters.title: Seylan Accelerate unveils interest-free salary advance

versions.title: Seylan Accelerate unveils interest-free salary advance
versions.deck: Seylan Bank PLC has strengthened its proposition for salaried professionals with the introduction of the ‘Seylan Accelerate Salary Advance Facility’, offering interest-free access to a portion of monthly income when financial needs arise.

clusters.title: People’s Bank presents 2025 Annual Report to President

versions.title: People’s Bank presents 2025 Annual Report to President
versions.deck: People’s Bank presented its 2025 Annual Report, themed “Rising Higher, Reaching Further”, to President Anura Kumara Dissanayake, at the Presidential Secretariat, in the presence of several distinguished senior State officials.

clusters.title: Fairfirst relocates Bandarawela branch to better serve customers

versions.title: Fairfirst relocates Bandarawela branch to better serve customers
versions.deck: Fairfirst Insurance has relocated its Bandarawela branch to new premises at No. 93, Nugasevana Super Market, Ettampitiya Road, Bandarawela, with the branch now open to customers. The move provides easier access for visitors and a refreshed service environment designed to improve the overall customer experience.

clusters.title: Bond yields remain stable amid subdued activity

versions.title: Bond yields remain stable amid subdued activity
versions.deck: The secondary Bond market continued to see yields holding broadly steady for a fourth consecutive session, reinforcing the ongoing consolidation phase.

clusters.title: DFCC Bank wins global award for Sri Lanka’s first listed Blue Bond

versions.title: DFCC Bank wins global award for Sri Lanka’s first listed Blue Bond
versions.deck: DFCC Bank PLC has been recognised at the Environmental Finance Sustainable Debt Awards 2026, winning the “Innovation – Use of Proceeds (Green Bond)” category for its DFCC Blue Bond 2025, Sri Lanka’s first listed blue bond.

ticker.title: My priority is to take Sri Lanka to top of the tree in world cricket – Kirsten
